parsed_review.summary: CP00-796 (RP26 Enterprise SaaS Hardening, risk B, 40 units, RP26.P03.M02.S15-RP26.P03.M04.S12) is a descriptor-only API shape/workflow pack and its pack-level descriptor-verified claim is well supported. Metadata is consistent across manifest, review-baseline, review-request, and the registry pack binding (next CP00-797 / RP26.P03.M04.S13). The deliverable distribution (claude_review 2, implementation 19, contract 9, security_audit 4, test 5, hermes_evidence 1 = 40) and micro-title distribution (Type And Shape 6, Primary Implementation 22, Secondary Workflow 12) match the registry requirements and the manifest unit list exactly; I traced the API_CONTRACT_ROWS/API_TYPE_ROWS/API_IMPLEMENTATION_ROWS slices to per-section unit titles. All 14 commands in command-evidence.json returned exit 0, covering every expected validation command (closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack/test/validate/spec:requirements/weighted/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00:control-plane/git diff --check) plus rp26:enterprise-saas:validate confirming pack CP00-796, next CP00-797/RP26.P03.M04.S13, unit_count 40. model.test.js carries three CP796 tests asserting coverage, runtime-closed descriptor/packets, and contract promotion, and npm test passed. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced both in code (ENTERPRISE_SAAS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION spread into every freezeResult; validateDescriptor rejects runtime execution, tenant/SSO/SCIM/secret payloads, permission/audit writes, and enterprise-trust claims) and in the contract cp796 projection/handoff. Authority boundaries hold: descriptor sets claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; Claude packet is read_only with promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false; Hermes evidence is descriptor-only with emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false (not embedded in product runtime). The pack does not over-claim: manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, and downstream adjudication/construction-inspection/claude-review-result are legitimately pending the receipt this review produces. Review-hardening preconditions are satisfied (claude-opus-4-8/effort max/read_only, tools Read,Grep,Glob, no forbidden tools, auth preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON, invalid_review_attempts empty). Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approval authority, consistent with the stated authority boundary. One non-blocking cosmetic observation about changed-file-scope.json labeling is recorded below. Note: source files were inspected via Read in line-ranges because Grep/Glob repeatedly timed out on the oversized registry.js (very long lines) and the 708KB command-evidence.json; verification relied on direct reads and the passing test/validator evidence.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies the pack's own evidence directory
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-796/ (the active pack's evidence directory) is listed both under untracked_implementation_files (line 173) and under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 181). The manifest worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved correctly does NOT list this directory as unrelated, so this is an internal labeling overlap in the generated review-harness artifact only. The authoritative scope fields (active_pack_implementation_refs and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted=[]) are correct, so pack scope integrity and closeout are unaffected. docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md is similarly listed in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, but it is correctly excluded from active pack scope.
findings.recommendation: In the changed-file-scope generator, exclude the active pack's own evidence directory (docs/closeout-packs/<pack>/) from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved so a file is classified as either active-implementation or unrelated, not both. Cosmetic; no closeout action required for CP00-796.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking cosmetic observation in a review-harness artifact; authoritative scope fields are correct. No change required before closeout.
